evals.prompt: I need to audit our React dashboard before launch next week. It's a data viz app with interactive charts, form inputs, and a dark mode toggle. Some users complained they couldn't navigate with keyboard only. How do I run a full WCAG AA audit including keyboard navigation to catch all accessibility issues?
evals.expected_output: Clear guidance to run 4-layer audit (Lighthouse via DevTools, tab through all controls, check a11y tree for labels/roles, verify 4.5:1 contrast). Specific evidence paths. Verdict template that says PASS or FAIL with numbered issues.

evals.prompt: After our refactor, I need to prove we didn't break accessibility. We have Lighthouse at 85 before the change. What baseline evidence do I capture, and how do I detect regressions in keyboard nav or screen reader experience?
evals.expected_output: Capture baseline: Lighthouse JSON, keyboard test findings table (lines 91-100 format), a11y tree snapshot. After refactor: re-run same 4 layers, compare side-by-side. Any keyboard navigation that worked before but fails now = REGRESSION = FAIL.
